# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use GuzzleHttp\Client;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function fetchPreview(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');

        if (empty($url)) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'URL is required'], 400);
        }

        $client = new Client();
        try {
            $response = $client->get($url, [
                'http_errors' => false,
                'timeout'     => 10.0,
            ]);

            if ($response->getStatusCode() == 200) {
                $html = $response->getBody()->getContents();

                // Extract meta tags
                $metaTitle = preg_match('/<title>(.*?)<\/title>/s', $html, $matches) ? $matches[1] : '';
                $metaDescription = preg_match('/<meta name="description" content="(.*?)" \/>/i', $html, $matches) ? $matches[1] : '';

                // Extract Open Graph tags
                $ogTitle = preg_match('/<meta property="og:title" content="(.*?)" \/>/i', $html, $matches) ? $matches[1] : '';
                $ogDescription = preg_match('/<meta property="og:description" content="(.*?)" \/>/i', $html, $matches) ? $matches[1] : '';
                $ogImage = preg_match('/<meta property="og:image" content="(.*?)" \/>/i', $html, $matches) ? $matches[1] : '';

                return response()->json([
                    'url' => $url,
                    'title' => !empty($ogTitle) ? $ogTitle : $metaTitle,
                    'description' => !empty($ogDescription) ? $ogDescription : $metaDescription,
                    'image' => $ogImage,
                ], 200);
            } else {
                return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch the webpage'], $response->getStatusCode());
            }
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Error fetching the URL'], 500);
        }
    }
}
